use tonic::Request;

async fn call_service(addr: &str, name: &str) -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
    let channel = tonic::transport::Channel::from_shared(addr.to_string())?
        .connect()
        .await?;
    let mut client = my_service::MyServiceClient::new(channel);
    let request = Request::new(my_service::HelloRequest { name: name.to_string() });
    let response = client.say_hello(request).await?;
    Ok(response.into_inner().message)
}

async fn batch_call(addresses: &[String], name: &str) -> Vec<String> {
    let mut results = Vec::new();
    for addr in addresses {
        let channel = tonic::transport::Channel::from_shared(addr.clone())
            .unwrap()
            .connect()
            .await
            .unwrap();
        let mut client = my_service::MyServiceClient::new(channel);
        let request = Request::new(my_service::HelloRequest { name: name.to_string() });
        let response = client.say_hello(request).await.unwrap();
        results.push(response.into_inner().message);
    }
    results
}

mod my_service {
    tonic::include_proto!("my_service");
    pub struct HelloRequest { pub name: String }
    pub struct HelloResponse { pub message: String }
    pub struct MyServiceClient<T>(T);
    impl<T> MyServiceClient<T> {
        pub fn new(_t: T) -> Self { Self(_t) }
        pub async fn say_hello(&mut self, _req: Request<HelloRequest>) -> Result<tonic::Response<HelloResponse>, tonic::Status> { todo!() }
    }
}
